identity.canonical_title: Quantum Harmonic Oscillator and Standing Waves: V(x)

theory.technical_interpretation: Quantum Harmonic Oscillator and Standing Waves: V(x) is an algebraic definition, written V(x) = (1)/(2) m ω^2 x^2. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from ω, m, x, defining V(x). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where in the quantum harmonic oscillator (QHO)
